subject: Astronomy & Space
title: How Many Earths Are Out There?
description: A micro-lesson that makes How Many Earths Are Out There? usable.

hook.text: Astronomers estimate that one in five Sun-like stars has a rocky planet in its habitable zone. That's billions of candidates.

buildup.text: Kepler data showed rocky planets are common. The Drake equation's biggest unknown is getting clearer.

discovery.text: Eta-Earth—the fraction of stars with Earth-like planets—is estimated at 10–20%. The galaxy may host 10 billion such worlds.

twist.text: Having a rocky planet in the zone doesn't mean it's habitable. Atmosphere, water, and magnetic fields all matter.

climax.text: Future missions will narrow the estimate. Each new planet tells us whether our kind of world is common or precious.

punchline.text: Ten billion possible Earths in our galaxy alone. The universe may be full of them.

quiz.question: What does 'eta-Earth' refer to in astronomy?
options: The estimated fraction of Sun-like stars with Earth-sized planets in habitable zones
The rate at which Earth-like planets lose their atmospheres
The distance ratio between Earth and the nearest habitable exoplanet
